description: Confounder: The model assumes the weight remains float32, but the weight was explicitly cast

_meta.explanation: The rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because the affine path does not cast back to input_dtype.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes the weight remains float32, but the weight was explicitly cast to float16, triggering the dtype conversion in the affine path
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because the affine path does not cast back to input_dtype.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
